controls.name: Release & version tuple discipline
controls.objective: Published artifacts MUST be traceable to an immutable version tuple (tag or commit).
controls.requirement: The operator SHALL publish evidence artifacts that are traceable to an immutable version tuple (a Git tag or commit SHA).
controls.evaluation: Verify that published manifests and referenced bundles include a tag or commit SHA and that the referenced repository state is retrievable.

controls.name: Machine-readable assurance profile published
controls.objective: Relying parties can fetch a machine-readable assurance posture declaration.
controls.requirement: The operator SHALL publish a TRQP Assurance Profile artifact conforming to the repository schema.
controls.evaluation: Validate the published profile against the schema and confirm required references exist for the target tier.

controls.name: Control satisfaction evidence bound to controls
controls.objective: Control claims are bound to objective evidence rather than prose.
controls.requirement: For AL2 and above, the operator SHOULD publish a Control Satisfaction Declaration that enumerates in-scope controls and links evidence references.
controls.evaluation: Validate the declaration schema; confirm control IDs exist in the control catalog; spot-check evidence references for accessibility.

controls.name: Lifecycle state is asserted and maintained
controls.objective: Lifecycle posture is explicit and updates are evidence-backed.
controls.requirement: For AL2 and above, the operator SHOULD publish a Lifecycle Assertion describing current lifecycle state and supporting evidence for state transitions.
controls.evaluation: Validate lifecycle assertion schema; confirm referenced evidence exists; confirm state aligns with operational posture.

controls.name: Recognition edges are scoped and evidence-linked
controls.objective: Recognition is expressed as signable, scoped assertions.
controls.requirement: Where registry recognition is claimed, the operator SHOULD publish Recognition Assertion artifacts with explicit scope and evidence references.
controls.evaluation: Validate recognition assertions and verify scope fields are present; confirm evidence references are resolvable.

controls.name: Revocation signals are discoverable and actionable
controls.objective: Trust-impacting changes can be communicated to relying parties.
controls.requirement: For AL4, the operator SHALL support certification revocation signaling and publish a revocation pointer from certification attestations.
controls.evaluation: Confirm existence of a revocation URI or bulletin and that it includes reason/effective time; verify consumers can discover it.

controls.name: Certification attestation is issued with assessor identity
controls.objective: Certification results can be consumed programmatically with accountability.
controls.requirement: When claiming certification, the issuer SHALL publish a Certification Attestation including assessor identity, scope, validity, and evidence references.
controls.evaluation: Validate attestation schema; confirm referenced control satisfaction declarations exist; check validity window fields are present.

controls.name: Example artifacts remain schema-valid in CI
controls.objective: Repo artifacts are trustworthy and do not drift from schemas.
controls.requirement: The repository SHALL validate JSON examples against schemas in CI and fail builds on drift.
controls.evaluation: Run CI workflow or execute tools/validate_examples.py locally; confirm failure on invalid example.
